meta.name: Okra + Goat Vegetable Soup
meta.subtitle: Hearty goat soup with okra and vegetables • pantry-friendly • freezer/batch cooking ready

source.notes: SSA baseline aligned with your existing direction: goat + okra + mixed vegetables with optional tomato, corn, and pasta/rice. Built for 'use what you have' logic and batch cooking.

yield.notes: Makes ~18–20 cups depending on vegetable volume and simmer.

minimum: large heavy pot or Dutch oven

tasteProfile.notes: Deep, brothy goat flavor with tomato-forward warmth (optional), thickened slightly by okra. Flexible vegetables for a hearty bowl.

ingredients.label: Goat meat, bone-in chunks (stew pieces)
ingredients.notes: About 2 lb. Shoulder/leg chunks work well. Bone-in adds flavor.

ingredients.label: Salt (for seasoning goat early)
ingredients.notes: About 1 teaspoon; adjust later based on broth.

ingredients.label: Black pepper (for seasoning goat early)
ingredients.notes: About 1/2 teaspoon.

ingredients.label: Neutral oil or olive oil
ingredients.notes: About 2 tablespoons.

ingredients.label: Yellow onion, diced
ingredients.notes: About 1 large.

ingredients.label: Carrots, diced
ingredients.notes: About 3 medium.

ingredients.label: Celery, diced (optional)
ingredients.notes: About 3 stalks. Can replace with bell pepper if preferred.

ingredients.label: Bell pepper, diced (optional)
ingredients.notes: Use instead of or in addition to celery.

ingredients.label: Garlic, minced
ingredients.notes: About 4 cloves.

ingredients.label: Tomato paste (optional, for depth)
ingredients.notes: About 2 tablespoons.

ingredients.label: Diced tomatoes (canned) (optional)
ingredients.notes: One 14–15 oz can. Makes it more tomato-forward.

ingredients.label: Water or broth (goat stock, beef stock, or chicken stock)
ingredients.notes: About 10 cups. Start with 8 cups and add more if needed.

ingredients.label: Dried thyme (or fresh thyme)
ingredients.notes: About 1 teaspoon dried.

ingredients.label: Smoked paprika (optional)
ingredients.notes: About 1 teaspoon. Adds smoked depth without bacon.

ingredients.label: Cayenne pepper (optional)
ingredients.notes: Pinch to taste.

ingredients.label: Okra, sliced (fresh or frozen)
ingredients.notes: About 4 cups sliced. Frozen works great.

ingredients.label: Potatoes, diced (optional)
ingredients.notes: About 3 medium. Adds body and makes it more filling.

ingredients.label: Sweet corn (optional)
ingredients.notes: Frozen or canned; adds sweetness and color.

ingredients.label: Green peas (optional)
ingredients.notes: Frozen peas are easiest; add near the end.

ingredients.label: Baby lima beans or black beans (optional)
ingredients.notes: About 1 1/2 cups cooked or one drained can.

ingredients.label: Macaroni (optional; cook in-soup or separately)
ingredients.notes: About 1 cup dry. For best leftovers, cook separately.

ingredients.label: Salt (to finish)
ingredients.notes: Adjust to taste at the end depending on broth and tomatoes.

ingredients.label: Lemon juice or vinegar (optional, brightness)
ingredients.notes: 1–2 teaspoons at the end helps balance okra + goat richness.

ingredients.label: Fresh parsley or green onion, chopped (optional garnish)

steps.title: Season goat and brown (optional but recommended)
instructions: Pat goat dry. Season with the early salt and pepper.
Heat oil in a large pot over medium-high heat.
Brown goat pieces in batches (do not overcrowd), 2–3 minutes per side. Transfer browned goat to a plate.

steps.title: Sweat aromatics
instructions: Reduce heat to medium. Add onion, carrots, and celery and/or bell pepper (if using).
Cook 6–8 minutes until softened, scraping browned bits from the pot.
Add garlic and cook 30 seconds until fragrant.

steps.title: Bloom tomato paste and spices (optional)
instructions: If using tomato paste, stir it in and cook 1–2 minutes to deepen flavor.
Stir in thyme, smoked paprika (optional), and cayenne (optional) for 30 seconds.

steps.title: Simmer goat until tender
instructions: Return goat to the pot. Add water/broth and bay leaf (optional).
If using diced tomatoes, add them now.
Bring to a gentle simmer, cover, and cook 60–75 minutes until goat is tender.

steps.title: Add vegetables (okra + hearty veg)
instructions: Stir in okra. If using potatoes or beans, add them now.
Simmer 15–20 minutes until potatoes are tender and okra has thickened the broth slightly.

steps.title: Optional: add corn/peas and pasta
instructions: If using corn and/or peas, add during the last 5 minutes to warm through.
If using macaroni: either cook it directly in the soup (8–10 minutes) or cook separately and add per bowl for best leftovers.

steps.title: Season and serve
instructions: Taste and adjust with finishing salt and pepper.
Add lemon juice or vinegar (optional) to brighten.

vegOptions: Add what you have: sweet corn, peas, lima beans, carrots, potatoes, diced tomatoes, bell peppers, greens (add at the end).
Frozen mixed vegetables work in a pinch—add during the last 10–15 minutes.
okraHandling: Okra thickens naturally. If you want less thick: rinse frozen okra, add later, and avoid aggressive stirring.
If you want thicker: simmer okra longer and stir more often.
goatTenderizing: If goat is tough, extend simmer by 15–30 minutes.
Pressure cooker option: cook goat in broth ~35 minutes at pressure, then finish soup on stovetop with vegetables.
starchOptions: Potatoes make it heartier; macaroni makes it 'stew-soup' and kid-friendly.
For gluten-free: use rice or GF pasta cooked separately.

bestPractices: For freezer: freeze without pasta for best texture; add fresh pasta when reheating.
Cool quickly; portion into 2-cup servings for easy meals.

storage.fridge: Refrigerate up to 4 days. Soup thickens; add broth/water when reheating.
storage.freezer: Freeze up to 3 months (best without pasta).
storage.reheat: Reheat gently to a simmer. Add a splash of broth/water to loosen.

audit.testNotes: Schema-compliant: schemaVersion+meta present; ingredients include id+label+amount; steps include id+title+instructions; uses only allowed top-level properties per current recipe.schema.json.
